


# 📘 **10.01 — Lab: Azure AD Connect & Hybrid Identity Setup**

*A step‑by‑step, production‑grade lab for building Hybrid Identity using Azure AD Connect, PHS/PTA, and Hybrid Join.*

This lab is built using verified Microsoft Learn documentation for Azure AD Connect, hybrid identity, and authentication methods.    [\[learn.microsoft.com\]](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/dns-and-ad-ds)

***

# 🏁 **Lab Goal (What You Will Build)**

You will configure a **Hybrid Identity environment** where:

*   On‑premises **Active Directory** synchronizes with **Microsoft Entra ID**
*   Users can sign in to cloud apps using **PHS or PTA**
*   Domain‑joined Windows devices become **Hybrid Azure AD joined**
*   Conditional Access & Identity Protection can be applied
*   Cloud authentication is ready for **Zero Trust**

Microsoft Learn confirms Azure AD Connect enables integration with authentication protocols and sync patterns for hybrid identity.    [\[learn.microsoft.com\]](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/dns-and-ad-ds)

***

# 🧪 **Lab Requirements**

### ✔ On‑Premises

*   1 Domain Controller (Windows Server 2016+)
*   1 Member Server (for Azure AD Connect)
*   At least 1 domain‑joined Windows 10/11 machine

### ✔ Cloud

*   Microsoft Entra ID tenant
*   Global Administrator role
*   Verified custom domain (recommended)
*   Internet access on AAD Connect server

### ✔ Accounts

*   Domain Admin (on‑prem)
*   Entra Global Admin

***

# 🏗 **1. Lab Topology (Diagram)**

![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

***

# 🛠 **2. Step 1 — Prepare the AD Environment**

### ✔ 2.1 Ensure Forest/Domain Health

Run:

```cmd
dcdiag /v
repadmin /replsummary
```

### ✔ 2.2 Verify Correct UPN Suffix

Users should have internet‑routable UPN:

**GOOD**

    user@contoso.com

**BAD**

    user@contoso.local

Add UPN suffix:

    Active Directory Domains and Trusts → Properties → UPN Suffixes

### ✔ 2.3 Clean Up Stale Objects

Remove:

*   Disabled users
*   Old computer accounts
*   Bad/duplicate SPNs

### ✔ 2.4 Enable Required Ports

Azure AD Connect outbound:

*   HTTPS 443
*   HTTP 80
*   Secure DNS

***

# 🔧 **3. Step 2 — Install Azure AD Connect**

### ✔ 3.1 Download

From Microsoft:\
<https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=47594>

### ✔ 3.2 Launch Setup

Choose:

    Customize (Recommended)

This enables Hybrid Join and PTA/PHS customization.

### ✔ 3.3 Select Authentication Method

You must choose **one**:

| Method                               | Notes                          |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |
| **Password Hash Sync (Recommended)** | Best for most orgs; cloud auth |
| **Pass-Through Authentication**      | Validates password on‑prem     |
| **Federation (ADFS)**                | Legacy; not recommended        |

Microsoft Learn confirms these three hybrid identity sign-in methods.    [\[learn.microsoft.com\]](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/plan/dns-and-ad-ds)

***

# 🔐 **4. Step 3 — Configure Directory Synchronization**

### ✔ 4.1 Connect to Active Directory

Provide Enterprise Admin credentials.

### ✔ 4.2 Connect to Entra ID

Provide Global Admin credentials.

### ✔ 4.3 Choose Sync Options

Enable:

*   **Password Hash Sync** or **PTA**
*   **Seamless SSO** (optional, for domain-joined PCs)
*   **Device Writeback** (needed for Hybrid Join WHfB scenarios)
*   **Group Filtering / OU Filtering**

### ✔ 4.4 Configure Domain/OU Filtering

Select only necessary OUs:

    OU=Users
    OU=Workstations
    OU=Servers
    OU=Service Accounts

### ✔ 4.5 Review & Install

Click **Install**.

Sync begins automatically.

***

# 🌩 **5. Step 4 — Enable Hybrid Azure AD Join**

Hybrid Join allows domain-joined devices to register in Entra ID.

### ✔ 5.1 In Azure AD Connect

Enable:

    Configure device options → Hybrid Azure AD Join

### ✔ 5.2 Select OS Version

*   Windows 10 or later
*   Windows Server (optional)

### ✔ 5.3 Configure SCP (Service Connection Point)

AAD Connect auto-writes this to AD.

### ✔ 5.4 Verify from client machine

Run:

```powershell
dsregcmd /status
```

Check:

    AzureAdJoined : YES
    DomainJoined : YES

***

# 🚀 **6. Step 5 — Test Authentication**

### ✔ 6.1 Test Cloud Sign-In

Go to <https://portal.office.com>\
Login using AD credentials.

### With PHS:

Cloud authenticates user.

### With PTA:

Password is validated on‑prem.

***

# 🔍 **7. Step 6 — Validate Sync & Hybrid Status**

### ✔ Users

Check:\
**Entra Portal → Users → Directory Synced = Yes**

### ✔ Devices

Check:\
**Entra Portal → Devices → Join Type = Hybrid Azure AD Joined**

### ✔ Sign-In Logs

View at:\
**Identity → Monitoring → Sign-In Logs**

***

# 🛡 **8. Step 7 — Apply Initial Security Controls**

Now that hybrid identity works, apply:

### ✔ Conditional Access

Policies:

*   Require MFA
*   Block legacy authentication
*   Require compliant device

### ✔ Identity Protection

Risk policies for:

*   User risk
*   Sign-in risk

### ✔ PIM (Privileged Identity Management)

Enforce JIT for admin roles.

***

# 🧪 **9. Optional Lab Extensions**

### 🧩 Add Cloud Kerberos Trust

Modern passwordless hybrid authentication.

### 🧩 Configure PTA Agents (multiple for HA)

For regulated industries.

### 🧩 Configure ADFS Decommission Plan

Move federation → PHS/PTA.

### 🧩 Integrate with Sentinel

Forward sign-in & audit logs.

***

# 🧵 **10. Troubleshooting Guide**

### ❌ **Hybrid Join Fails**

Check:

*   SCP exists
*   AD Connect device sync enabled
*   Time sync between endpoints

### ❌ **Password Doesn’t Sync**

Check:

*   PHS enabled
*   No sync errors
*   UPN suffix matches verified domain

### ❌ **User Cannot Sign-In**

Check:

*   Licenses assigned
*   UPN matches Entra domain
*   Successful 4624 logon on DC

***

# 🎯 **Summary**

By the end of this lab you have:

✔ Installed Azure AD Connect\
✔ Synced AD identities to Entra ID\
✔ Enabled PHS/PTA authentication\
✔ Configured Hybrid Azure AD Join\
✔ Verified user/cloud sign-in works\
✔ Prepared the environment for Conditional Access\
✔ Created the foundation for Zero Trust Identity

This completes your baseline Hybrid Identity deployment experience.

***
